expectations.evidence: All required section headers are present: Problem, Solution, Scope, Technical Approach, Risks, Alternatives, Verification.
No matches for file-line patterns like 'file.ts:123' were found.
No fenced code blocks with ts/tsx/typescript/javascript/js language tags appear in the spec.
Spec is 91 lines, which is under the 120-line maximum.

Technical Approach defines core triggers (assignment/status/completion transitions), backend persistence ownership, frontend state location (dedicated Zustand store), and user flow via bell/dropdown and read actions.

Behavior triggers are specified: emit after successful task mutations, status notifications on semantic transitions, completion notifications only when newly complete, and pull-based UI refresh on initial load/post-mutation/periodic refresh.
First sentences in each Technical Approach paragraph state major decisions directly (domain model location, emission strategy, watch trigger ownership, frontend store strategy).
Uses repeated meta-phrases at least 3 times (e.g., 'The design decision is...', 'The non-obvious choice here is...', 'The non-obvious choice is...'), exceeding the allowed threshold.

Technical Approach includes algorithmic implementation detail (e.g., 'state-transition logic anchored to before/after task values at write time') and route-level mechanism specifics rather than pure behavior/policy.
Three failure modes are present and each includes a policy choice (idempotent creation, coalescing unread status changes, preserving records with board-level fallback links).
Out-of-scope list names realistic adjacent creep items: email/push channels, realtime delivery, notification preferences, and full comments feature.
